LAW AND MOTION 302, RULING: A R G U E D. AT THE CONCLUSION OF ARGUMENT, THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING ON DEFENDANT ESTATE OF BUCKMINSTER FULLER'S MOTION FOR RELIEF. THE COURT GRANTS THE MOTION TO VACATE THE DISMISSAL AND STIPULATION. ESTATE ESTABLISHES SUFFICIENT SURPRISE, AND RELIEF IS WARRANTED UNDER ROTH V. MORTON'S CHEFS SERVS. (1985) 173 CAL.APP.3D 380, 385. ESTATE MUST FILE AN ANSWER WITHIN 10 DAYS. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: PETER J. BUSCH; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY C. VAUGHN, CSR # 6185

APPEARANCES - ATTY. ARTHUR SCHWARTZ OF GORDON & REES LLP, TEL. NO. 875-3161 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. THOMAS A. COHEN, TEL. NO. 777-1997 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT.

RULING - A R G U E D; PLAINTIFF ALTERRA EXCESS SURPLUS INSURANCE COMPANY'S Mtn For Judgmnt On The Pleadings is granted without leave to amend. Plaintiff owed no duty to defend the underlying federal action based on the intellectual property exclusion. See Aroa Marketing, Inc. v. Hartford Ins. Co. of Midwest (2011) 198 Cal.App.4th 781, 788. Arthur Schwartz, Esq. of Gordon & Rees, Tel. 415.986.5900, for plaintiff and moving party;

RULING: A R G U E D. At the conclusion of argument, the Court adopted its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFFs ALTERRA SPECIALTY INSURANCE & ALTERRA EXCESS SURPLUS INSURANCE COMPANY's Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings is denied without prejudice in light of the ruling on the Estate's motion for relief. Order signed in open Court. Judge: Peter J. Busch; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185

Amended Notice Of Mtn For Relief
Off Calendar May-08-2013 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Thomas Cohen, Esq. of the Law Offices of Thomas A. Cohen, Tel. 415.777.1997, for defendant and moving party;
Arthur Schwartz, Esq. of Gordon & Rees, Tel. 415.986.5900, for plaintiff and opposing party.

RULING: A R G U E D. At the conclusion of argument, the Court adopted its tentative ruling on DEFENDANT ESTATE OF BUCKMINSTER FULLER's Motion For Relief. The Court grants the motion to vacate the dismissal and stipulation. Estate establishes sufficient surprise, and relief is warranted under Roth v. Morton's Chefs Servs. (1985) 173 Cal.App.3d 380, 385. Estate must file an answer within 10 days. Order signed in open Court. Judge: Peter J. Busch; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185
